full_text:
- Jaipur Hottest Night, Barmer Nears 46°C | Rajasthan Heatwave & Rain Outlook
Rajasthan faces dual alert of rain and heatwave:Jaipur logs hottest night, Barmer temperature nears 46°C; weather outlook ahead
- Copy link
People in Rajasthan, who had been struggling with intense heat and humidity, got some relief on Saturday as light rain and hailstorm were reported in several districts.
According to the Meteorological Centre in Jaipur, the change in weather is due to a western disturbance, which is likely to remain active over the state for the next three days. During this period, some areas may witness dust storms, rain and hail.
A yellow alert for rain has been issued in two districts, while 13 districts remain under heatwave alert. Despite the brief relief, temperatures continue to stay high. Barmer recorded the highest temperature at 45.7°C, while Jaipur saw the hottest night of the season.
Temperatures above 40°C across all cities
In the last 24 hours, every city in Rajasthan recorded temperatures above 40°C, showing the widespread impact of the heatwave. Cities like Jaisalmer, Jodhpur, Kota, Bikaner and Barmer recorded maximum temperatures between 44°C and 46°C.
Some areas also reported high humidity, making the weather more uncomfortable for residents.
Jaipur records hottest night; Kota sees peak summer heat
Jaipur remained extremely hot during the day, with the temperature reaching 41.8°C. At night, the minimum temperature was recorded at 31.4°C, which is 7.4 degrees above normal, making it the hottest night of the season.
In Kota, the maximum temperature touched 44.3°C, the highest this season. Due to the intense heat, streets remained largely empty during the afternoon. Authorities have set up 11 relief shelters with coolers and basic facilities for the public.
Mixed weather: rain in some areas, no relief in others
Sikar saw a sudden change in weather in the evening, with brief rain followed by heavy showers, giving temporary relief from the heat.
However, places like Alwar and Ajmer continued to experience hot winds and discomfort, even after cloud cover appeared.
In Jodhpur, temperatures remained high at 44.4°C, though light winds in the evening brought slight relief. Udaipur also recorded its highest temperature of the season at 42°C.
What lies ahead
The Meteorological Department has said that weather fluctuations will continue for the next three days, with chances of rain and storms in some areas. However, heatwave conditions are likely to persist in many districts, and people are advised to take precautions.
